title: gradient of a lambda capturing LOCAL scalars: LLVM 'PtrToInt source must be pointer' (ptrtoint on tagged_value struct)
goal: Noesis nested-AD report Issue 4 (HIGH). A differentiated lambda that closes over TOP-LEVEL scalars compiles fine, but one that closes over LOCAL/parameter scalars fails LLVM module verification: 'PtrToInt source must be pointer' — codegen emits `ptrtoint %eshkol_tagged_value %a to i64` on the captured local, i.e. it ptrtoint's the whole 16-byte tagged_value STRUCT (not a pointer). Confirmed repro: `(define (loss a t x) (let ((d (- x t))) (* a (* d d)))) (define (step a t w0) (gradient (lambda (x) (loss a t x)) w0)) (step 8.0 5.0 7.0)` => ERROR PtrToInt source must be pointer (%a, %t). The SAME code with a,t as top-level defines compiles and gives 32 (exact). So the AD closure-capture/lifting path treats a captured LOCAL tagged value as a pointer and ptrtoints it, whereas top-level globals are unboxed/loaded correctly.
next_actions: Find where the AD/gradient codegen materializes captured free variables of the differentiated lambda. The top-level path loads/unboxes the tagged value; the local-capture path emits ptrtoint on the %eshkol_tagged_value struct directly.

Fix the capture handling so a captured LOCAL tagged value is loaded from its slot and unboxed (extractvalue data field {4} / extractDoubleFromTagged) exactly like the global case — never ptrtoint a struct.
Add a test: gradient of a lambda capturing local params equals the top-level-capture result exactly (32 for the repro), and the nested case (outer gradient over step) also works.
acceptance: (step 8.0 5.0 7.0) with a,t LOCAL => 32 (matches top-level-capture version), no LLVM verification error, -r AND AOT
outer gradient over a step that captures locals works
campaign_payoff: Lets differentiated functions capture local/param scalars (natural module structure) instead of forcing module-level cells; removes a HIGH-severity codegen failure blocking Noesis's natural code organization.
